mnemonics: ABCs of Abstraction: Abstract away Boilerplate Code by Simplifying
keyPoints: Abstraction hides complex implementation details and shows only essential features.
Abstract classes cannot be instantiated and can have both abstract and concrete methods.
Interfaces provide 100% abstraction (until Java 8) and define a contract that implementing classes must follow.
commonMistakes: Trying to instantiate an abstract class directly with the 'new' keyword.
Forgetting to implement all abstract methods from a parent abstract class or interface.
Creating overly complex abstract structures when a simple class would suffice, over-engineering the solution.
